slides.title: Pronouns and Who Questions
speakerNotes: You already know how to say hello and introduce your name. But how do you talk about other people? Today you learn the words for I, you, he, she — and how to ask who someone is.
Use basic pronouns and ask who someone is using คือ and ใคร in short controlled exchanges.

textBlocks.heading: Lesson focus
lines: You can use basic pronouns and ask who someone is.
Point, identify, and ask who someone is in a mini-dialogue.

visualStrategy.onScreenGoal: Introduce the lesson clearly and set the recording tone.
teachingVisuals: Pronouns and Who Questions
First Contact and Courtesy
teacherCues: Use the deck title as the recording opener.
visualStrategy.rationale: The opener should establish identity and objective without visual clutter.

slides.title: What you will learn
speakerNotes: You can use basic pronouns and ask who someone is.
You can recycle recent vocabulary while using identity question with ใคร in short controlled output.
Point, identify, and ask who someone is in a mini-dialogue.
Distinguish pronouns and who-questions in short exchanges.

textBlocks.heading: Objectives
lines: You can use basic pronouns and ask who someone is.
You can recycle recent vocabulary while using identity question with ใคร in short controlled output.
Point, identify, and ask who someone is in a mini-dialogue.
Distinguish pronouns and who-questions in short exchanges.

visualStrategy.onScreenGoal: Make the lesson goals visible before the teaching slides begin.
teachingVisuals: You can use basic pronouns and ask who someone is.
You can recycle recent vocabulary while using identity question with ใคร in short controlled output.
Point, identify, and ask who someone is in a mini-dialogue.

teacherCues: Keep the slide readable from a distance.
visualStrategy.rationale: Objectives are clearer as cards than as decorative visuals.

slides.title: I and you — the first pronouns
speakerNotes: Let's start with the most important words in any conversation — I and you.
In Thai, there are different words for I. The general one is ฉัน chǎn. If you are male and want to sound polite, use ผม phǒm instead.
For you, the safe polite word is คุณ khun. You already know this from คุณชื่ออะไร — what is your name. คุณ means you.
So if someone asks who you are, you can say ฉันชื่อ... chǎn chûue... — I am called... Or if you are male: ผมชื่อ... phǒm chûue...

textBlocks.heading: Key language
lines: ฉัน | chǎn | I (general)
ผม | phǒm | I (male, polite)
คุณ | khun | you

textBlocks.heading: Practice
lines: Listen and repeat: ฉัน (chǎn), ผม (phǒm), คุณ (khun). Say each one twice.
Pause-and-produce: I say a situation — male speaker or female speaker. You say the right word for I before I show the answer.
Response-building: I say คุณชื่ออะไร (khun chûue à-rai). You answer with ฉันชื่อ... or ผมชื่อ... and your name.

visualStrategy.onScreenGoal: Show the three pronoun cards clearly with gender note on ผม.
teachingVisuals: Three pronoun cards: ฉัน, ผม, คุณ with transliteration and meaning
Gender indicator next to ผม showing male-polite usage
teacherCues: Point to each pronoun as you model it. Gesture to yourself for ฉัน/ผม, gesture to the learner for คุณ.
visualStrategy.rationale: Pronouns are abstract — text cards with clear labels teach better than images.

slides.title: He, she, and this — pointing to people
speakerNotes: Now let's talk about other people. เขา khǎo means he or she. Thai uses the same word for both.
นี่ nîi means this. And คือ khuue means is. So when you put them together: นี่คือ... nîi khuue... means this is...
Try it: นี่คือไมค์ nîi khuue Mike — this is Mike. Or: เขาคือครูนาย khǎo khuue khruu Naai — he is Teacher Naai.
คือ is special — it connects a name or identity to a person. Think of it as the equals sign.

textBlocks.heading: Key language
lines: เขา | khǎo | he / she
นี่ | nîi | this
คือ | khuue | is (identity)

textBlocks.heading: Practice
lines: Listen and repeat: เขา (khǎo), นี่ (nîi), คือ (khuue). Now together: นี่คือ... (nîi khuue...).
Substitution drill: I give a name. You say นี่คือ... with that name. Example: Mike → นี่คือไมค์.
Pause-and-produce: I point to someone and say เขาชื่ออะไร (khǎo chûue à-rai). You answer เขาชื่อ... with a name.

visualStrategy.onScreenGoal: Show เขา, นี่, คือ as individual cards, then the chunk นี่คือ... as a combined card.
teachingVisuals: Three word cards: เขา, นี่, คือ
One chunk card: นี่คือ... with arrow showing how the parts combine
teacherCues: Point to a person or object when modelling นี่คือ... to make the demonstrative concrete.
visualStrategy.rationale: Abstract grammar words — text cards are clearer than images.

slides.title: Ask who someone is
speakerNotes: Now the question word: ใคร khrai means who. In Thai, the question word stays where the answer would go.
So to ask who is he or she, you say: เขาคือใคร khǎo khuue khrai. Literally: he/she is who?
And to confirm: คุณคือ...ใช่ไหม khun khuue...châi mǎi — you are... right? You already know ใช่ from lesson 1.
Try the full exchange: เขาคือใคร — เขาคือไมค์ — ไมค์ใช่ไหม — ใช่ครับ.

textBlocks.heading: Key language
lines: ใคร | khrai | who
เขาคือใคร | khǎo khuue khrai | who is he/she?
คุณคือ...ใช่ไหม | khun khuue...châi mǎi | you are...right?

textBlocks.heading: Practice
lines: Listen and repeat: ใคร (khrai). Now the full question: เขาคือใคร (khǎo khuue khrai).
Response-building: I ask เขาคือใคร. You answer เขาคือ... with any name you choose.
Pause-and-produce: I say a name. You build the confirmation: คุณคือ[name]ใช่ไหม before I show it.

thaiFocus.thai: คุณคือ...ใช่ไหม

visualStrategy.onScreenGoal: Show ใคร as a standalone question word, then the two chunks as complete patterns.
teachingVisuals: Question word card: ใคร
Two chunk cards showing the full patterns with slots for names
teacherCues: Model the rising intonation on ใคร clearly. Point to an imaginary person when asking เขาคือใคร.
visualStrategy.rationale: Question patterns — text-first teaching is clearest.

slides.title: Put it together — a mini identity exchange
speakerNotes: Let's use everything together. Imagine you are at a gathering and you see someone you don't know.
You turn to your friend and ask: เขาคือใคร khǎo khuue khrai — who is that?
Your friend answers: นี่คือแอน nîi khuue Anne. เขามาจากประเทศไทย khǎo maa-jàak prà-thêet thai — she comes from Thailand.
You confirm: แอนใช่ไหม Anne châi mǎi — Anne, right? And your friend says: ใช่ครับ châi khráp — yes.

textBlocks.heading: Key language
lines: เขาคือใคร | khǎo khuue khrai | who is he/she?
นี่คือ... | nîi khuue... | this is...
เขามาจากประเทศไทย | khǎo maa-jàak prà-thêet thai | he/she comes from Thailand

textBlocks.heading: Practice
lines: Role swap: I play the friend. You ask เขาคือใคร and I answer. Then you confirm with ใช่ไหม.
Substitution drill: I change the name and country. You rebuild the full exchange each time.
Production challenge: introduce yourself using ฉัน or ผม, then ask who the person next to you is.

visualStrategy.onScreenGoal: Show a short exchange as a dialogue ladder with pronoun and identity patterns highlighted.
teachingVisuals: Dialogue ladder with 4 turns showing the full exchange
Highlighted slots where names and countries can be substituted
teacherCues: Model the full exchange first, then have the learner take turns speaking each role.
visualStrategy.rationale: The dialogue ladder itself is the visual — no images needed.

slides.title: Roleplay
speakerNotes: At a gathering, you ask your friend who someone is, learn their name and country, and confirm.
You | ขอโทษครับ เขาคือใครครับ | khǎaw-thôot khráp khǎo khuue khrai khráp | Excuse me, who is he/she?
Friend | นี่คือแอนค่ะ | nîi khuue Anne khâ | This is Anne.
You | เขามาจากประเทศอะไรครับ | khǎo maa-jàak prà-thêet à-rai khráp | What country is she from?
Friend | เขามาจากประเทศไทยค่ะ | khǎo maa-jàak prà-thêet thai khâ | She's from Thailand.
You | คนไทยใช่ไหมครับ | khon thai châi mǎi khráp | A Thai person, right?
Friend | ใช่ค่ะ | châi khâ | Yes.

textBlocks.heading: Dialogue
lines: You | ขอโทษครับ เขาคือใครครับ | khǎaw-thôot khráp khǎo khuue khrai khráp | Excuse me, who is he/she?
Friend | นี่คือแอนค่ะ | nîi khuue Anne khâ | This is Anne.
You | เขามาจากประเทศอะไรครับ | khǎo maa-jàak prà-thêet à-rai khráp | What country is she from?
Friend | เขามาจากประเทศไทยค่ะ | khǎo maa-jàak prà-thêet thai khâ | She's from Thailand.
You | คนไทยใช่ไหมครับ | khon thai châi mǎi khráp | A Thai person, right?
Friend | ใช่ค่ะ | châi khâ | Yes.

thaiFocus.thai: ขอโทษครับ เขาคือใครครับ
thaiFocus.translit: khǎaw-thôot khráp khǎo khuue khrai khráp

thaiFocus.thai: นี่คือแอนค่ะ
เขามาจากประเทศอะไรครับ
เขามาจากประเทศไทยค่ะ
คนไทยใช่ไหมครับ

visualStrategy.onScreenGoal: Support the roleplay recording with clean turn-taking.
teachingVisuals: At a gathering, you ask your friend who someone is, learn their name and country, and confirm.
visualStrategy.rationale: The dialogue itself is the teaching focus.

slides.title: Recap
speakerNotes: How do you say I if you are male? ผม (phǒm).
How do you say this is...? นี่คือ... (nîi khuue...).
How do you ask who someone is? เขาคือใคร (khǎo khuue khrai).
How do you confirm someone's identity? คุณคือ...ใช่ไหม (khun khuue...châi mǎi).
What does คือ mean? It connects a person to their identity — is.

textBlocks.heading: What you can now do
lines: How do you say I if you are male? ผม (phǒm).
How do you say this is...? นี่คือ... (nîi khuue...).
How do you ask who someone is? เขาคือใคร (khǎo khuue khrai).
How do you confirm someone's identity? คุณคือ...ใช่ไหม (khun khuue...châi mǎi).
What does คือ mean? It connects a person to their identity — is.

visualStrategy.onScreenGoal: Finish with a simple, memorable checklist.
teachingVisuals: How do you say I if you are male? ผม (phǒm).
How do you say this is...? นี่คือ... (nîi khuue...).
How do you ask who someone is? เขาคือใคร (khǎo khuue khrai).
How do you confirm someone's identity? คุณคือ...ใช่ไหม (khun khuue...châi mǎi).
What does คือ mean? It connects a person to their identity — is.
teacherCues: Use this slide as the summary.
Keep the ending calm and uncluttered.
visualStrategy.rationale: A recap slide should be compact and memorable.

slides.title: Closing
speakerNotes: You can now say I, you, he/she, point to someone and say who they are, and ask who someone is.
Next: First Contact and Courtesy continues.

textBlocks.heading: Takeaway
lines: You can now say I, you, he/she, point to someone and say who they are, and ask who someone is.
Next: First Contact and Courtesy continues.

visualStrategy.onScreenGoal: Give Nine a clean final slide to close the recording.
teachingVisuals: You can now say I, you, he/she, point to someone and say who they are, and ask who someone is.
Next: First Contact and Courtesy continues.
teacherCues: Use the closing as the final spoken wrap-up.
visualStrategy.rationale: The closing slide is a presenter anchor, not a new teaching beat.
